reason: A score of 70/100 is broadly consistent with fan and critical consensus. Dumbledore is widely considered one of the more significantly altered characters in the film adaptations - the infamous 'calmly' scene in GoF, the omission of his OotP confession explaining his distance from Harry, the loss of his internal conflict and backstory (Grindelwald, his past), and his death scene being changed all support low motivation and arc scores (11/25 each). The personality score (19/25) is slightly generous given widespread criticism of Gambon's erratic, aggressive portrayal vs the book's calm, calculating Dumbledore, but defensible since the core wisdom and mentor role came through. The narrative role score (14/25) seems the most questionable - Dumbledore remains central to the plot in the films, so a score that low may be harder to justify purely on role grounds (though omitted plot threads like the Horcrux explanation depth and his manipulation of Harry being unexplained do weaken his agency). Overall 70 is a reasonable, slightly generous total.
